content.full_text:
building
industry
association
political
action
committee

March 14, 1990

OFFICERS
Michael Keston
Chairman
Kenneth W. Willis
Treasurer

AREA VICE CHAIRMEN
Gordon Tippell
Orange County
Robert Henninger
Riverside County
Ian D. Hedberg
Ventura/Santa Barbara Counties
Phil Nicholson
Los Angeles County
Paul Friedman
San Bernardino County

EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE
Anieloye Julio
Norman Titcher
Richard Petersen
Buddy View
Ted Dalton
Jim Prevett
Greater Los Angeles
Bill Lockett
Brian Carlisle
Orange County
Bill York
Phil Bettencourt
Los Angeles County East
John Hawkins
Riverside County
Bob Nastase
Randall Hall
Santa Barbara County
Wayne Colmer
Ventura County
Elaine Freeman
Government Affairs Council
DeVore Anderson
Sherman Gardner
Roy Humphreys
President, BIA/SC
Jack Shine
Vice Chairman, NAHB Build PAC

DIRECTORS AT LARGE
Jim Smirl
Don Neffensen
Peter Ochs

Ms. Alice Hughes
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Hughes:

As a follow-up to our telephone conversation today, please
acknowledge that the following is how we should handle reporting and
recording of our planned fund raising event on May 10, 1990.

BACKGROUND:

We are a general purpose recipient PAC. We maintain a
Candidate Account and a Non-Candidate Account. We use the
Non-Candidate Account to pay for administrative expenses
and to make contributions to ballot measures and issues.
It will be stated on our flyers that contributions will
be allocated according to the schedule below.

o First $1,900 from any individual or corporation:
1. 50% to our CANDIDATE ACCOUNT
2. 50% to CBIA/PAC (separate from our PAC)

o Any amount in excess of $1,900:
3. 70% to our NON-CANDIDATE ACCOUNT
4. 30% to BILD Foundation (not a PAC)

RECORDKEEPING & REPORTING:

1. Upon receipt of contribution checks, form letters will
be sent to contributors informing them of how their
contributions will be allocated. Those letters will
have to be signed by them and returned to us to
indicate their approval of the allocation.

2. Contribution checks shall be deposited to our
Candidate Account. Any amount not allocated to the
Candidate Account shall be immediately paid to the
CBIA/PAC, Non-Candidate Account, or BILD Foundation as
the case may be. For convenience, we will cut
reimbursement checks on a weekly basis.

3. We do not have to report to FPPC any monies that are
not allocated for our Candidate Account and Non-
Candidate Account.

1571 Beverly Boulevard • Los Angeles, California 90026 • (213) 250-8965 • ID# 741-733

Alice Hughes
FPPC
Page 2
_________________________

4. We do not have to report to FPPC any reimbursements
to CBIA/PAC and BILD Foundation. However, we have to
keep accurate records for future audit. We will also
attach a list of contributors to the CBIA/PAC
reimbursement check. CBIA/PAC will have to report the
contributions to the FPPC.

If you have any question or want some changes made, give me a call
at 213-250-8965. Please signify your acknowledgment by returning
this letter to me with your signature on the space provided below.

Thank you.


Sincerely,

[signature]
Larry Lopez
Recordkeeper
ACKNOWLEDGED:

_________________________
Alice Hughes

sections.facts:
We are a general purpose recipient PAC. We maintain a Candidate Account and a Non-Candidate Account. We use the Non-Candidate Account to pay for administrative expenses and to make contributions to ballot measures and issues. It will be stated on our flyers that contributions will be allocated according to the schedule below.

o First $1,900 from any individual or corporation:
    1. 50% to our CANDIDATE ACCOUNT
    2. 50% to CBIA/PAC (separate from our PAC)

o Any amount in excess of $1,900:
    3. 70% to our NON-CANDIDATE ACCOUNT
    4. 30% to BILD Foundation (not a PAC)

RECORDKEEPING & REPORTING:

1. Upon receipt of contribution checks, form letters will be sent to contributors informing them of how their contributions will be allocated. Those letters will have to be signed by them and returned to us to indicate their approval of the allocation.

2. Contribution checks shall be deposited to our Candidate Account. Any amount not allocated to the Candidate Account shall be immediately paid to the CBIA/PAC, Non-Candidate Account, or BILD Foundation as the case may be. For convenience, we will cut reimbursement checks on a weekly basis.

3. We do not have to report to FPPC any monies that are not allocated for our Candidate Account and Non-Candidate Account.

4. We do not have to report to FPPC any reimbursements to CBIA/PAC and BILD Foundation. However, we have to keep accurate records for future audit. We will also attach a list of contributors to the CBIA/PAC reimbursement check. CBIA/PAC will have to report the contributions to the FPPC.

If you have any question or want some changes made, give me a call at 213-250-8965. Please signify your acknowledgment by returning this letter to me with your signature on the space provided below.

Thank You.
